arts.ttl: 'I felt that' - Jamie Carragher delivers honest Liverpool assessment after Champions League exit
arts.sum: Liverpool have been knocked out of the Champions League after taking Paris Saint-Germain to penalties in the Round of 16

arts.ttl: Why Ousmane Dembele Takes Penalties With His Weak Foot
arts.sum: PSG and France star Ousmane Dembele explained why he opts to take penalties with his weaker right foot

arts.ttl: Arne Slot pays tribute to 'incredible' Liverpool as he rues side's 'bad luck'
arts.sum: Liverpool's European ambitions were left in the dust as the Premier League's runaway leaders were dumped out of the competition by PSG on penalties after losing the second leg at Anfield

arts.ttl: David Moyes can't resist Liverpool dig as they're knocked out of Champions League
arts.sum: Everton will take on Liverpool in the Premier League in April and manager David Moyes was asked about their rivals ahead of the Reds' Champions League elimination

arts.ttl: PSG Fan Used ‘Never-Seen-Before’ Tactic During Shootout vs Liverpool
arts.sum: A PSG supporter used a megaphone to distract Liverpool players in their Champions League round of 16 match.

arts.ttl: 'Wasted': Arne Slot told he made one costly mistake that didn't help Liverpool vs PSG at all
Thierry Henry snubs Mohamed Salah to name surprise former Premier League star as favourite for Ballon d'Or - and hints at Liverpool forward's reliance on penalties
arts.sum: Salah's form has helped Liverpool establish a 15 point lead over challengers Arsenal at the top of the Premier League table, but the Reds were knocked out of the Champions League on Tuesday.

arts.ttl: All-action Vitinha embodies new PSG as Luis Enrique shifts the culture | Jonathan Liew
arts.sum: The PSG midfielder covered every blade of grass at Anfield as his side frustrated Liverpool for 120 minutes Afterwards, they carried on running. They had run for two hours, run themselves into the ground, run themselves delirious, but at the moment of triumph the players of Paris Saint-Germain somehow managed to find a few more yards in them. Ran towards their fans in the corner, ran in wild circles, tore across the Anfield grass as if it were the Champs-Élysées. One man did not run. As Désiré Doué’s penalty hit the net, Vitinha simply crumpled, his legs finally giving way, his last drop of energy exhausted. Eventually, with his teammates still celebrating 70 yards away, he hauled himself to his feet and was the first to commiserate with Liverpool’s beaten players. Then, after they had dispersed, he simply stood in the centre circle for a few moments, as if finally claiming the turf for which he had spent the whole night fighting. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: PSG's dream of conquering Europe now looks a case of 'when' not 'if', writes LEWIS STEELE, as Luis Enrique's side down Liverpool after moving on from the Messi and Neymar era
arts.sum: LEWIS STEEL : This team proved at Anfield that they are the cream of the crop in Europe. Not proven winners like Real Madrid but a fearless side who played like they belong at the very elite level.

arts.ttl: PSG boss Luis Enrique dismisses Liverpool theory and makes Champions League vow
arts.sum: Luis Enrique masterminded Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League victory over Liverpool, beating the Arne Slot's men on the so-called 'lottery' of penalties to book their place in the quarter-finals
